qas.q: Classification structure
qas.a: Career progression who is a specialties, authorize prefixes, reporting Identifiers, special duty Identifiers, Air Force specialty descriptions, and there assigned Air Force specialty codes.

qas.q: ALPH Prefix
qas.a: For ability, skill, special qualification, or system designator not restricted to a single AFSC.

qas.q: Reporting identifier
qas.a: Recording identifiers identify authorizations and individual enlisted Airman who for any reason Are not identifiable in the classification structure and for whom specialty discriptions are not written.

qas.q: Awarded air force specialty code
qas.a: An AFSC awarded to an individual as primary, second, third, fourth fourth AFSC after certification of ability to perform in positions of an Air Force specialty at a certain skill level. Fourth AFSC applies only to enlisted personnel.

qas.q: CAFSC
qas.a: A management tool used to make enlisted Airman assignments, to assist in determining training requirements, and to consider individuals for promotion.

qas.q: CEM Code
qas.a: Five digit code ending in 00 to identify master sergeant and chief Master Sergeant select as cottonwood does managers involved highly technical skills and in broad areas of managerial at competence

qas.q: 6 possible skill levels
qas.a: Helper, apprentice, journey man, craftsman, superintendent, Chief enlisted manager.

When making changes to policy, guidence or procedures found in official pubs , what are the acceptable methods of change?
qas.a: Re-write, interim change, administrative change, AF policy memo, guidence memo.

qas.q: What if the difference between permanent and temporary pub changes?
qas.a: Permanent is effective for life of published version. Temp expires after a period of time.

qas.q: What is difference between integrated change and attached change?
qas.a: Integrated is inserted into the text of original doc. Attached is a separate doc that sits on top of the original and notifies the reader that the original has changed.
